statuses.supported: Implemented with positive tests for the repo-owned CrossGL contract.
statuses.partial: Implemented for common cases, but known target features or edge cases are missing.
statuses.diagnostic: The backend emits a deterministic diagnostic or fallback instead of full target code.
statuses.unsupported: The backend intentionally does not support this capability yet.
statuses.validated_rejection: The backend rejects this shape with an explicit validation error.
statuses.unknown: Not audited yet. Do not treat this as supported.

features.name: CrossGL to target code generation
features.description: Emit target-language source or target representation from CrossGL.

features.name: Native source to CrossGL
features.description: Parse backend source language and regenerate CrossGL.

features.name: Native lexer coverage
features.description: Tokenize backend source language constructs used by the native parser.

features.name: Native preprocessor handling
features.description: Handle native preprocessor directives, includes, defines, and conditional compilation where the backend language exposes them.

vulkan.notes: Vulkan source imports run a GLSL-compatible preprocessor before lexing, expanding object-like and function-like defines, include files, and conditional compilation while enforcing #version placement. Remaining native directives such as #version and #extension are accepted and filtered before parsing, so preprocessed Vulkan GLSL/SPIR-V-style sources parse without leaking directive tokens into the AST.

cuda.notes: CUDA source imports run a native C/CUDA-style preprocessor before lexing, expanding object-like and function-like defines, local include files, and conditional compilation. Unresolved system includes such as CUDA runtime headers are preserved as preprocessor nodes for parser/codegen handling, while #error/#warning and malformed conditional directives emit deterministic diagnostics.

hip.notes: HIP source imports run a native C/HIP-style preprocessor before lexing, expanding object-like and function-like defines, local include files, and conditional compilation. Unresolved system includes such as HIP runtime headers are preserved as parser preprocessor nodes for backend conversion, while #error/#warning and malformed conditional directives emit deterministic diagnostics.

mojo.notes: Mojo has no C-style preprocessor surface in this frontend; the supported source-handling contract treats # lines as comments and parses native import/from-import declarations as the inclusion mechanism. Tests cover hash-prefixed directive-like lines being ignored without leaking macro tokens and imports continuing to parse normally.

rust.notes: Rust has no C-style preprocessor; native source-control constructs are represented as attributes and use items. The Rust backend tokenizes and parses cfg-style attributes on functions and use statements, preserves attribute arguments for downstream handling, and parses simple, grouped, glob, alias, and public use imports.

slang.notes: Slang source imports run a native C/HLSL-style preprocessor before lexing, expanding object-like and function-like defines, local include files, and conditional compilation. Remaining directive lines such as pragmas are filtered before parsing, and #error/#warning or malformed conditional directives emit deterministic diagnostics.

features.name: Vertex stage
features.description: CrossGL vertex entry point lowering.

cuda.notes: CUDA has no native raster vertex stage, so CrossGL vertex entry points lower to CUDA device functions with deterministic CrossGL stage metadata. Vertex return semantics such as gl_Position/SV_Position and vertex input semantics such as gl_VertexID/SV_InstanceID are preserved as metadata comments with stage/type/direction validation, and optional nvcc smoke coverage verifies the generated device signatures when nvcc is available.

hip.notes: HIP has no native raster vertex stage, so CrossGL vertex entry points lower to HIP device functions with deterministic CrossGL stage metadata. Vertex return semantics such as gl_Position/SV_Position and vertex input semantics such as gl_VertexID/SV_InstanceID are preserved as metadata comments with stage/type/direction validation, and optional hipcc smoke coverage verifies generated device signatures when hipcc is available.

mojo.notes: Mojo vertex entry points lower to compile-oriented Mojo functions with deterministic CrossGL shader-stage metadata. Vertex return semantics such as gl_Position/SV_Position and vertex input semantics such as gl_VertexID/SV_InstanceID and TEXCOORD/NORMAL/POSITION parameters are preserved as metadata comments with stage/type/direction validation, full vertex input structs lower to Mojo signatures, and optional Mojo compile coverage validates generated vertex/fragment packages when the Mojo toolchain is available.

rust.notes: Rust vertex entry points lower to #[vertex_shader] functions with deterministic entry names, including duplicate CrossGL stage main names. Vertex input/output structs, stage-local structs and uniform statics, direct return semantics such as gl_Position/SV_Position, and builtin vertex parameters such as gl_VertexID/SV_InstanceID are preserved as generated Rust types or deterministic CrossGL metadata comments with stage/type/direction validation. Rust source roundtrips preserve vertex stage entries through the backend translate API, and smoke coverage validates representative generated vertex modules with rustc when available.

features.name: Fragment/pixel stage
features.description: CrossGL fragment entry point lowering.

cuda.notes: CUDA has no native raster fragment stage, so CrossGL fragment entry points lower to CUDA device functions with deterministic CrossGL stage metadata. Fragment return semantics such as gl_FragColor/SV_Target and gl_FragDepth, fragment input semantics such as gl_FragCoord/SV_Position and gl_FrontFacing/SV_IsFrontFace, and struct-member render-target/depth semantics are preserved as metadata comments with stage/type/direction validation. Optional nvcc smoke coverage verifies representative generated fragment device signatures when nvcc is available.

hip.notes: HIP has no native raster fragment stage, so CrossGL fragment entry points lower to HIP device functions with deterministic CrossGL stage metadata. Fragment return semantics such as gl_FragColor/SV_Target and gl_FragDepth, fragment input semantics such as gl_FragCoord/SV_Position and gl_FrontFacing/SV_IsFrontFace, and struct-member render-target/depth semantics are preserved as metadata comments with stage/type/direction validation. Optional hipcc smoke coverage verifies representative generated fragment device signatures when hipcc is available.

mojo.notes: Mojo fragment entry points lower to compile-oriented fragment_main signatures with deterministic CrossGL shader-stage metadata. Fragment return semantics such as gl_FragColor/SV_Target and gl_FragDepth, fragment input semantics such as gl_FragCoord/SV_Position, gl_FrontFacing/SV_IsFrontFace, gl_PointCoord, and TEXCOORD parameters, and struct-member render-target/depth semantics are preserved as Mojo comments or field annotations with stage/type/direction validation. Optional Mojo package smoke coverage validates representative generated vertex/fragment modules when the Mojo toolchain is available.

rust.notes: Rust fragment entry points lower to #[fragment_shader] functions with deterministic entry names, including duplicate CrossGL stage main names. Fragment return semantics such as gl_FragColor/SV_Target and gl_FragDepth, fragment input semantics such as gl_FragCoord/SV_Position and gl_FrontFacing/SV_IsFrontFace, multiple render-target structs, and struct-member color/depth semantics are preserved as generated Rust types or deterministic CrossGL metadata comments with stage/type/direction validation. Backend Rust-to-CrossGL conversion preserves fragment shader entries, and smoke coverage validates representative generated fragment modules with rustc when available.

features.name: Compute stage
features.description: CrossGL compute entry point lowering.

slang.notes: Compute entry points lower to Slang shader entries with HLSL-compatible numthreads defaults and parsed local-size layouts.

features.name: Geometry stage
features.description: Geometry shader stage and primitive stream shape validation.

metal.notes: Metal has no traditional native geometry shader stage, so CrossGL geometry entry points lower to deterministic Metal helper functions with CrossGL geometry-stage metadata and compile-oriented PointStream/LineStream/TriangleStream placeholders. The lowering preserves geometry input primitive qualifiers and output stream element types in metadata, emits stream Append/RestartStrip calls against placeholder stream helpers, requires positive maxvertexcount attributes, and validates point/line/triangle/adjacent input primitive array arity.

vulkan.notes: SPIR-V geometry stages lower to Geometry entry points with OpCapability Geometry and stage execution modes for input primitive topology, output topology, OutputVertices/maxvertexcount, and invocation count. Geometry stage builtins are emitted into the entry interface, EmitVertex and EndPrimitive lower to OpEmitVertex and OpEndPrimitive, and invalid geometry metadata, wrong-stage stream calls, and stream builtin arity errors produce deterministic diagnostics.

cuda.notes: CUDA geometry stages lower to deterministic __device__ helper functions with CrossGL geometry-stage metadata and compile-oriented PointStream/LineStream/TriangleStream placeholders. The lowering preserves geometry input primitive qualifiers and output stream element types in metadata, emits stream Append/RestartStrip calls against placeholder helpers, requires positive maxvertexcount attributes, and validates point/line/triangle/adjacent input primitive array arity.

hip.notes: HIP geometry stages lower to deterministic __device__ helper functions with CrossGL geometry-stage metadata and compile-oriented PointStream/LineStream/TriangleStream placeholders. The lowering preserves geometry input primitive qualifiers and output stream element types in metadata, emits stream Append/RestartStrip calls against placeholder helpers, requires positive maxvertexcount attributes, and validates point/line/triangle/adjacent input primitive array arity.

mojo.notes: Mojo geometry stages lower to deterministic compile-smoke helper functions with CrossGL geometry-stage metadata and PointStream/LineStream/TriangleStream placeholders. The lowering preserves geometry input primitive qualifiers and output stream element types in metadata, emits stream Append/RestartStrip calls against placeholder helpers, requires positive maxvertexcount attributes, and validates point/line/triangle/adjacent input primitive array arity.

rust.notes: Rust geometry stages lower to deterministic compile-smoke functions with CrossGL geometry-stage metadata and PointStream/LineStream/TriangleStream placeholders. The lowering preserves geometry input primitive qualifiers and output stream element types in metadata, emits stream Append/RestartStrip calls against placeholder helpers, requires positive maxvertexcount attributes, validates point/line/triangle/adjacent input primitive array arity, and is covered by rustc smoke compilation.

slang.notes: Maps geometry blocks to Slang geometry entry points, lowers geometry system-value builtins, preserves semantic aliases, emits maxvertexcount attributes, preserves input primitive qualifiers and PointStream/LineStream/TriangleStream signatures, lowers stream Append/RestartStrip calls, and validates stream method arity, element types, inout stream qualifiers, input primitive presence, and primitive array arity.

features.name: Tessellation stages
features.description: Tessellation control/hull and tessellation evaluation/domain stages.

opengl.notes: GLSL tessellation control/evaluation stages lower native layout metadata and tessellation builtins, while unsupported patchconstantfunc metadata is rejected with a deterministic diagnostic instead of emitting invalid GLSL.

metal.notes: Metal tessellation control and evaluation stages lower to deterministic helper functions with CrossGL tessellation metadata and compile-oriented InputPatch/OutputPatch placeholders. The lowering preserves domain, partitioning, output topology, outputcontrolpoints, patchconstantfunc, maxtessfactor, patch parameter element/count metadata, and tessellation helper semantics while validating patch signatures and domain-location shapes.

vulkan.notes: SPIR-V codegen lowers tessellation control and evaluation stages to TessellationControl/TessellationEvaluation entry points with deterministic execution modes from stage layouts, stage attributes, and top-level qualified FunctionNode metadata. It preserves output vertices, domain, spacing, vertex order, patch-constant data, patch builtins, and patch parameter interfaces while validating tessellation metadata and patch contexts.

cuda.notes: CUDA tessellation control and evaluation stages lower to deterministic __device__ helper functions with CrossGL tessellation metadata and compile-oriented InputPatch/OutputPatch placeholders. The lowering preserves outputcontrolpoints, domain, partitioning, output topology, and patchconstantfunc metadata, maps patch parameter types to placeholder templates, requires positive outputcontrolpoints on control stages, requires valid domain metadata on evaluation stages, and validates partitioning values.

hip.notes: HIP tessellation control and evaluation stages lower to deterministic __device__ helper functions with CrossGL tessellation metadata and compile-oriented InputPatch/OutputPatch placeholders. The lowering preserves outputcontrolpoints, domain, partitioning, output topology, and patchconstantfunc metadata, maps patch parameter types to placeholder templates, requires positive outputcontrolpoints on control stages, requires valid domain metadata on evaluation stages, and validates partitioning values.

mojo.notes: Mojo tessellation control and evaluation stages lower to deterministic helper functions with CrossGL tessellation metadata and compile-oriented InputPatch/OutputPatch placeholders. The lowering preserves outputcontrolpoints, domain, partitioning, output topology, and patchconstantfunc metadata, maps patch parameter types to placeholder containers, requires positive outputcontrolpoints on control stages, requires valid domain metadata on evaluation stages, and validates partitioning values.

rust.notes: Rust tessellation control and evaluation stages lower to deterministic helper functions with CrossGL tessellation metadata and compile-oriented InputPatch/OutputPatch placeholders. The lowering preserves outputcontrolpoints, domain, partitioning, output topology, patchconstantfunc, and patch parameter metadata, maps patch parameter types to placeholder containers, requires positive outputcontrolpoints on control stages, requires valid domain metadata on evaluation stages, validates partitioning values, and rejects invalid patch generic shapes/counts.

slang.notes: Maps tessellation_control and tessellation_evaluation to Slang hull/domain stages, lowers tessellation system values, tessellation-factor semantics, gl_in/gl_out patch rewrites, patch-constant functions, stage attributes, and validates hull/domain patch shapes plus required outputcontrolpoints/domain metadata.

features.name: Mesh/task/amplification stages
features.description: Modern mesh-style stage entry points.

directx.notes: Entry points lower to HLSL mesh/amplification shader attributes with deterministic stage names, numthreads from local-size layouts or @numthreads, mesh output topology attributes, mesh output signatures for vertices/indices/primitives, mesh payload parameters, groupshared DispatchMesh payload forwarding, and native SetMeshOutputCounts/DispatchMesh calls. The backend validates output roles/counts/topology, system-value parameter types, SetMeshOutputCounts arity/bounds/stage/control-flow/write ordering, DispatchMesh arity/group-count bounds/stage/control-flow, payload address space, payload type matching, and required payload dispatches.

opengl.notes: Entry points and combined-stage names lower with GL_EXT_mesh_shader enablement, local-size layouts, mesh output topology/count layouts, taskPayloadSharedEXT payload storage, DispatchMesh payload assignment, native EmitMeshTasksEXT/SetMeshOutputsEXT calls, mesh output signatures for vertices/indices/primitives, helper forwarding through SetVertex/SetPrimitive, and topology-specific primitive index builtins. The backend validates layout aliases/conflicts, positive count constants, DispatchMesh arity/count/payload shapes, mesh output parameter shapes/counts, SetMeshOutputCounts arity/count bounds, output literal/dynamic indices, whole-output assignment shapes, helper arity/value types, and expression-context misuse.

metal.notes: Entry points lower to valid Metal object/mesh function attributes with deterministic names, max_total_threads_per_threadgroup from local-size layouts or explicit attributes, generated or explicit mesh output signatures/topology, SetMeshOutputCounts primitive counts, whole vertex/index/primitive output writes, single-member output member writes, multi-member partial output writes through accumulators, object/mesh payload parameters, DirectX-style @mesh_payload parameters, and DispatchMesh payload argument forwarding through object_data payloads. The backend validates literal output-count bounds, write ordering, helper ordering, if/switch branch dominance, loop-exit dominance for post-branch/output writes, payload address spaces, dispatch payload source/type shapes, grid arguments, and generated name collisions.

vulkan.notes: SPIR-V codegen lowers mesh and task stages with SPV_EXT_mesh_shader capability/extension metadata, MeshEXT/TaskEXT entry points, local-size execution modes, mesh output limits/topology, SetMeshOutputCounts, SetVertex/SetPrimitive, mesh output signature parameters, per-primitive and builtin outputs, helper forwarding, taskPayloadSharedEXT payload interfaces, and DispatchMesh payload operands while validating arity, scalar/non-negative counts, declared output limits, output assignment shapes, invalid members/components, ambiguous payload targets, and wrong-stage mesh helpers.

cuda.notes: CUDA mesh, task, object, and amplification stages lower to deterministic __device__ helper functions with CrossGL mesh/task metadata and compile-oriented placeholder hooks. Mesh lowering preserves numthreads, output topology, max_vertices, max_primitives, and SetMeshOutputCounts calls; task/object/amplification lowering preserves numthreads and DispatchMesh payload calls. The backend validates output topology, positive local-size metadata, SetMeshOutputCounts arity, and DispatchMesh stage/arity.

hip.notes: HIP mesh, task, object, and amplification stages lower to deterministic __device__ helper functions with CrossGL mesh/task metadata and compile-oriented placeholder hooks. Mesh lowering preserves numthreads, output topology, max_vertices, max_primitives, and SetMeshOutputCounts calls; task/object/amplification lowering preserves numthreads and DispatchMesh payload calls. The backend validates output topology, positive local-size metadata, SetMeshOutputCounts arity, and DispatchMesh stage/arity.

mojo.notes: Mojo mesh, task, object, and amplification stages lower to compile-oriented Mojo functions with deterministic CrossGL mesh/task metadata and placeholder helpers. Mesh lowering preserves output topology, max vertex/primitive limits, mesh payload/output roles, SetMeshOutputCounts calls, and task/object/amplification DispatchMesh calls while validating topology, positive limits, helper stage context, and helper arity.

rust.notes: Rust mesh, task, object, and amplification stages lower to deterministic functions with CrossGL mesh/task metadata and compile-oriented placeholder helpers. Mesh lowering preserves output topology, mesh payload/output roles, max vertex/primitive limits, SetMeshOutputCounts calls, and task/object/amplification DispatchMesh calls while validating topology, positive local-size metadata, output parameter roles/counts, helper stage context, and helper arity/count types.

slang.notes: Entry points lower to Slang mesh/amplification shader attributes with deterministic duplicate-entry names, local-size layouts or @numthreads attributes, quoted outputtopology attributes, native SetMeshOutputCounts/DispatchMesh calls, mesh output signatures for vertices/indices/primitives, helper forwarding, mesh payload parameters, and DispatchMesh payload arguments. The backend validates intrinsic arity/stage/value contexts, count argument types, output roles/counts/topology, SetMeshOutputCounts ordering and duplicates, output write order and literal indices, payload parameter shapes, payload lvalue/type matching, and invalid helper payload arguments.

features.name: Ray tracing stages
features.description: Ray generation, hit, miss, callable, and intersection stages.

directx.notes: Ray generation, closest-hit, any-hit, miss, callable, and intersection stages lower to HLSL library shader attributes with deterministic stage names. Payload, callable-data, and hit-attribute stage parameters map to native HLSL inout/in signatures with role, order, and type validation. RaytracingAccelerationStructure resources and RayDesc operands preserve HLSL ray-tracing types and bindings, TraceRay, CallShader, ReportHit, IgnoreHit, and AcceptHitAndEndSearch lower to native HLSL calls with stage, arity, operand, ray-flag, and instance-mask validation, and RayQuery methods lower to native HLSL methods with receiver/argument validation and result type inference.

opengl.notes: Ray generation, closest-hit, any-hit, miss, intersection, and callable stages lower with deterministic combined-stage entry names or target-stage main entry points and GL_EXT_ray_tracing enablement. Acceleration-structure resources, shaderRecordEXT buffers, rayPayloadEXT/rayPayloadInEXT, hitAttributeEXT, callableDataEXT/callableDataInEXT, TraceRay, CallShader, IgnoreHit, ReportHit, and ray hit position-fetch extension scoping are covered by GLSL codegen tests.

metal.notes: Ray generation payloads lower to device references; acceleration structures and intersection_function_table resources lower to Metal raytracing buffer parameters; full TraceRay calls lower to ray plus intersector intersection queries with xcrun metal3 coverage for helper-threaded calls, instance masks, primitive_acceleration_structure shape, single compatible intersection_function_table dispatch, and thread-local payload forwarding. Unsupported TraceRay payload forms, such as missing compatible tables or non-thread payload parameters, emit compile-safe diagnostics. RayQuery declarations and methods lower to compile-safe CglRayQuery placeholder helpers because Metal has no HLSL-style RayQuery object. CallShader lowers to visible_function_table dispatch for explicit tables or a single global table; visible_function_table and intersection_function_table ray-generation parameters lower with compiler-validated buffer bindings; hit, miss, any-hit, and callable stages lower to visible functions with ray_data payload/callable-data references; intersection stages lower to Metal intersection attributes for triangle and bounding-box functions. Metal-prohibited arrayed visible_function_table and intersection_function_table globals or parameters emit compile-safe diagnostics while scalar table globals, parameters, explicit callable dispatch, and intersection-table TraceRay paths remain compiler validated.

vulkan.notes: Ray generation, intersection, any-hit, closest-hit, miss, and callable stages lower to SPIR-V KHR ray tracing entry points with validator-backed RayTracingKHR capability and SPV_KHR_ray_tracing extension coverage. Acceleration-structure resources preserve descriptor metadata and participate in entry-point interfaces, ray payload, callable data, and hit attribute values are bridged through RayPayloadKHR, CallableDataKHR, and HitAttributeKHR storage variables, launch and hit builtins lower to SPIR-V builtins, TraceRay, CallShader, ReportHit, IgnoreHit, and AcceptHitAndEndSearch lower to native KHR instructions, and RayQuery methods lower to KHR ray-query instructions including triangle position fetch coverage.

cuda.notes: Ray generation, closest-hit, any-hit, miss, callable, and intersection stages lower to CUDA device entry functions with deterministic CrossGL ray-stage metadata comments. RaytracingAccelerationStructure resources preserve resource metadata, ray payload, hit attribute, callable data, launch, hit-t, and hit-kind semantics are preserved as deterministic comments or placeholder hooks, TraceRay, CallShader, ReportHit, IgnoreHit, and AcceptHitAndEndSearch lower to CUDA hook calls, and RayQuery methods lower to deterministic CUDA query hooks with optional nvcc smoke coverage.

hip.notes: Ray generation, closest-hit, any-hit, miss, callable, and intersection stages lower to HIP device entry functions with deterministic CrossGL ray-stage metadata comments. RaytracingAccelerationStructure resources preserve resource metadata, ray payload, hit attribute, callable data, launch, hit-t, and hit-kind semantics are preserved as deterministic comments or placeholder hooks, TraceRay, CallShader, ReportHit, IgnoreHit, and AcceptHitAndEndSearch lower to HIP hook calls, and RayQuery methods lower to deterministic HIP query hooks with optional hipcc smoke coverage.

mojo.notes: Ray generation, closest-hit, any-hit, miss, callable, and intersection stages lower to Mojo entry functions with deterministic CrossGL stage metadata comments. RaytracingAccelerationStructure resources preserve resource metadata, ray payload, hit attribute, callable data, launch, hit-t, and hit-kind semantics are preserved as deterministic comments or placeholder hooks, TraceRay, CallShader, ReportHit, IgnoreHit, and AcceptHitAndEndSearch lower to Mojo hook calls, and RayQuery methods lower to deterministic Mojo query hooks with package compile-smoke coverage.

rust.notes: Ray generation, closest-hit, any-hit, miss, callable, and intersection stages lower to compileable Rust entry functions with deterministic CrossGL ray-stage metadata comments. RaytracingAccelerationStructure resources preserve resource metadata comments, ray payload, hit attribute, callable data, launch, hit-t, and hit-kind semantics are preserved as deterministic comments or runtime hooks, and TraceRay, CallShader, ReportHit, IgnoreHit, and AcceptHitAndEndSearch lower to Rust hook calls with rustc smoke coverage.

slang.notes: Ray entry points lower to Slang shader stage attributes with deterministic stage names, ray built-ins lower to Slang intrinsics, payload, hit attribute, and callable data stage interfaces lower to Slang parameter directions, native TraceRay, CallShader, ReportHit, IgnoreHit, and AcceptHitAndEndSearch calls are stage/shape/type validated, RaytracingAccelerationStructure resources preserve deterministic vk/register bindings, and RayQuery methods emit native Slang calls with receiver/argument validation and result type inference. Optional slangc smoke coverage validates generated ray interfaces and a bound TraceRay/CallShader pipeline path when slangc is available.

features.name: Stage parameter semantics
features.description: Input parameter semantics and target builtin attributes.

vulkan.notes: Global sampled textures, samplers, storage images, resource arrays, and cbuffers share deterministic DescriptorSet/Binding assignment with explicit @set/@group/@binding handling and duplicate binding rejection.

cuda.notes: CUDA preserves builtin stage parameter semantics as deterministic CrossGL comments, maps common vertex/fragment/compute system-value aliases, and validates builtin parameter stage, type, duplicate system values, and output-only misuse deterministically. Optional nvcc smoke coverage runs when available.

hip.notes: HIP preserves builtin stage parameter semantics as deterministic CrossGL comments, maps common vertex/fragment/compute system-value aliases, and validates builtin parameter stage, type, duplicate system values, and output-only misuse deterministically. Optional hipcc smoke coverage runs when available.

mojo.notes: Stage parameter semantics are preserved as deterministic stage-aware Mojo parameter comments for compile-smoke output, common vertex/fragment/compute builtin and generic semantics are mapped, duplicate parameter semantics, output-only input misuse, builtin stage misuse, and builtin parameter type mismatches are rejected deterministically.

rust.notes: Rust preserves struct, direct return, and builtin stage parameter semantics as deterministic generated comments for compile-smoke output, maps compute/vertex/fragment system-value aliases, and validates builtin parameter stage, type, duplicate system values, and output-only misuse deterministically.

slang.notes: Stage parameter semantics map CrossGL/GLSL builtins and HLSL SV semantics to Slang system values across vertex, fragment, compute, geometry, tessellation, mesh, amplification, and ray interfaces; builtin stage input parameters validate stage, type, duplicate system values, and output-only misuse deterministically.

features.name: Direct function return semantics
features.description: Function return semantics, wrapper output structs, and invalid-return validation.

vulkan.notes: Direct entry-point return semantics lower to SPIR-V Output variables or BuiltIn Position/FragDepth outputs, fragment color returns use deterministic Location decorations, invalid builtin type/stage/input-only return semantics are rejected, and generated modules validate through spirv-as/spirv-val when available.

cuda.notes: Direct return semantics are preserved as deterministic CrossGL return-semantic comments, returned struct members preserve semantic comments, and builtin direct and struct return semantics validate stage and type compatibility. Optional nvcc smoke coverage runs when available.

hip.notes: Direct return semantics are preserved as deterministic CrossGL return-semantic comments, returned struct members preserve semantic comments, and builtin direct and struct return semantics validate stage and type compatibility. Optional hipcc smoke coverage runs when available.

mojo.notes: Direct staged function return semantics are preserved as deterministic Mojo metadata comments, returned struct members preserve semantic comments, builtin direct and struct return semantics validate stage and type compatibility, and optional Mojo package coverage verifies generated stage entry signatures remain valid.

rust.notes: Rust direct return semantics are preserved as deterministic CrossGL return-semantic comments, returned struct members preserve semantic comments, builtin direct and struct return semantics validate stage and type compatibility, and rustc smoke coverage verifies the generated Rust remains valid.

slang.notes: Direct return semantics map to Slang system values, fragment color/depth returns can be rewritten through output structs, invalid return semantics are rejected, stage/execution marker metadata is filtered so it does not become a return semantic, and optional slangc smoke coverage verifies the generated direct-return ABI.

features.name: Struct member semantics
features.description: Input/output struct member semantics and builtin type validation.

vulkan.notes: Struct member semantics lower to SPIR-V member BuiltIn decorations for Position and SV_Position aliases, mesh-output struct members receive deterministic Location decorations, duplicate input/output locations are rejected, and generated modules validate through the SPIR-V tools smoke checks when available.

cuda.notes: Struct member semantics are preserved as deterministic CUDA field comments, GLSL builtins and HLSL SV aliases map to CUDA semantic comments, builtin output semantic types are validated on struct members and direct returns, returned struct semantics validate stage compatibility, and input-only builtins are rejected for outputs.

hip.notes: Struct member semantics are preserved as deterministic HIP field comments, GLSL builtins and HLSL SV aliases map to HIP semantic comments, builtin output semantic types are validated on struct members and direct returns, returned struct semantics validate stage compatibility, and input-only builtins are rejected for outputs.

mojo.notes: Struct member semantics are preserved as deterministic Mojo field comments, HLSL SV aliases map to Mojo semantic comments, builtin output semantic types are validated, struct-return builtin semantics validate stage compatibility, and input-only builtins are rejected for outputs.

rust.notes: Struct member semantics are preserved as Rust field comments, GLSL builtins and HLSL SV aliases map to Rust semantic comments, builtin output semantic types are validated on struct members and direct returns, returned struct semantics validate stage compatibility, and input-only builtins are rejected for outputs.

slang.notes: Struct member semantics map CrossGL/GLSL builtins and HLSL SV semantics to Slang system values across vertex, fragment, tessellation, and stage-interface structs; builtin output semantic types are validated on struct members and direct returns, struct-return semantics validate stage compatibility, input-only builtins are rejected for outputs, and stage/execution attributes are filtered from semantic lowering.

features.name: Explicit and automatic resource bindings
features.description: Resource binding/register/location assignment and collision avoidance.

vulkan.notes: Global sampled textures, samplers, storage images, resource arrays, and cbuffers share deterministic DescriptorSet/Binding assignment with explicit @set/@group/@binding handling and duplicate binding rejection.

cuda.notes: Global sampled textures, samplers, storage images, structured-buffer/resource-array declarations, GLSL buffer blocks, and cbuffers emit deterministic CrossGL resource metadata comments with explicit @set/@group/@binding and HLSL register-space preservation, automatic per-namespace binding assignment, fixed resource-array range reservation, duplicate/range-overlap diagnostics, and CUDA query sidecar metadata forwarding.

hip.notes: Global sampled textures, samplers, storage images, structured-buffer/resource-array declarations, GLSL buffer blocks, and cbuffers emit deterministic CrossGL resource metadata comments with explicit @set/@group/@binding and HLSL register-space preservation, automatic per-namespace binding assignment, fixed resource-array range reservation, duplicate/range-overlap diagnostics, and HIP query sidecar metadata forwarding.

rust.notes: Global texture, sampler, image, structured-buffer/resource-array, and cbuffer declarations emit deterministic CrossGL resource metadata comments with explicit @set/@group/@binding and HLSL register-space preservation, automatic per-namespace binding assignment, and duplicate/range-overlap diagnostics.

slang.notes: Global resources and cbuffers receive deterministic automatic [[vk::binding(binding, set)]] and HLSL register(space) decorations, explicit @set/@group/@space/@binding and register metadata is preserved on globals, cbuffers, arrays, acceleration structures, storage images, samplers, sampled textures, and resource parameters, fixed resource arrays reserve binding ranges, and conflicting binding/register, set/space, duplicate, and range-overlap metadata raises deterministic diagnostics.

features.name: Constant/uniform buffers
features.description: CrossGL cbuffer/uniform block lowering.

vulkan.notes: CrossGL cbuffers lower to SPIR-V Uniform blocks with Block, member Offset, matrix stride, array stride, descriptor set/binding decorations, bare/member access lowering, duplicate binding rejection, and spirv-as/spirv-val coverage.

cuda.notes: CrossGL cbuffers lower to CUDA constant-memory declarations with flattened scalar/vector/matrix member storage, deterministic resource binding metadata comments, automatic and explicit binding allocation, duplicate binding diagnostics, and compile-smoke coverage when nvcc is available.

features.name: Structured/storage buffers

features.name: Texture and sampler object model
features.description: Represent combined and separate texture/sampler models without binding collisions.

directx.notes: Covers separate texture and sampler declarations, implicit sampler threading, comparison sampler role separation, arrayed samplers, register-space inheritance, and collision-free binding namespaces.

opengl.notes: Covers combined sampler emission, source sampler elision, shadow-compare coordinate construction, sampler/image binding independence, arrayed sampler filtering, and collision-free binding namespaces.

metal.notes: Covers separate texture and sampler declarations, implicit sampler threading, comparison sampler role separation, arrayed samplers, and collision-free texture/sampler/buffer binding namespaces.

Explicit sampler operands are accepted for source compatibility while sampling uses the combined sampled-image operand, matching the repo-owned Vulkan contract. Descriptor set/binding allocation uses Vulkan's single per-set namespace across textures, samplers, images, buffers, and cbuffers, with duplicate explicit bindings rejected.

cuda.notes: Covers separate texture and sampler declarations, explicit sampler operands for CUDA texture/gather calls, sampler parameters, collision-free texture/sampler binding namespaces, and deterministic shadow/comparison sampler diagnostics. CUDA texture objects own sampler state, so explicit sampler operands are accepted for source compatibility while the generated texture fetch uses the texture object coordinates.

hip.notes: Covers separate texture and sampler declarations, explicit sampler operands for HIP texture calls, HLSL sampled-texture and sampler-state aliases, resource-array sampler indexing, and collision-free texture/sampler binding namespaces. mojo.notes: Covers separate texture and sampler declarations, split-sampler texture calls through Mojo placeholder helpers, HLSL sampled-texture and sampler-state aliases, shadow sampler result typing, resource-array sampler indexing, independent texture/sampler binding namespaces, and duplicate binding diagnostics within each namespace. Mojo models the CrossGL texture/sampler object contract for compile-smoke output; real GPU sampler filtering and target-native resource binding ABI remain placeholder semantics.

rust.notes: Covers separate texture and sampler declarations, split-sampler texture calls through Rust placeholder helpers, comparison sampler role separation, arrayed samplers, independent texture/sampler binding namespaces, and duplicate binding diagnostics within each namespace. Rust models the CrossGL texture/sampler object contract for compile-smoke output; real GPU sampler filtering and target-native resource binding ABI remain placeholder semantics.

slang.notes: Covers CrossGL sampled textures as Slang combined Sampler* resources, standalone sampler declarations as SamplerState resources, explicit sampler operands for source compatibility, fixed and expression-sized texture/sampler/shadow-sampler arrays, shadow compare/gather calls, independent Slang t/s/u register namespaces, explicit register-space preservation, automatic binding allocation, and same-namespace duplicate/range-overlap diagnostics. features.name: Resource memory qualifiers

cuda.notes: readonly/writeonly/readwrite access qualifiers are tracked for CUDA storage images, StructuredBuffer/RWStructuredBuffer, append/consume buffers, ByteAddressBuffer/RWByteAddressBuffer, and GLSL buffer-block placeholder resources, with deterministic invalid read/write/append/consume/atomic diagnostics. hip.notes: readonly/writeonly/readwrite access qualifiers are tracked for HIP storage images, StructuredBuffer/RWStructuredBuffer, ByteAddressBuffer/RWByteAddressBuffer, and GLSL buffer-block placeholder resources, with deterministic invalid read/write/atomic diagnostics. rust.notes: For the repo-owned compile-oriented Rust contract, readonly/writeonly/readwrite map StructuredBuffer/RWStructuredBuffer and ByteAddressBuffer/RWByteAddressBuffer declarations to placeholder access types, storage images preserve load/store helper shape, GLSL buffer arrays pick readonly vs writable resources, and GLSL buffer-block placeholders preserve readonly/writeonly metadata with deterministic invalid read/write diagnostics. coherent/globallycoherent, volatile, and restrict have no native Rust placeholder effect and do not leak as invalid Rust attributes.

slang.notes: Storage-image, StructuredBuffer, append/consume structured-buffer, ByteAddressBuffer, GLSL buffer-block, and resource-parameter readonly/writeonly/readwrite access metadata is tracked for Slang resources, with deterministic invalid read/write/append/consume/atomic diagnostics. coherent/globallycoherent lower to Slang globallycoherent on writable UAV-style resources; volatile/restrict are preserved only where Slang has an emitted resource form and otherwise do not leak as invalid semantics.

cuda.notes: Covers CUDA regular, offset, typed HLSL Texture.Load, array, 1D, 2D, 3D, cube/cube-array diagnostics, and multisample texel fetch/query forms with deterministic coordinate, offset, resource-role, and target-shape diagnostics.

hip.notes: Covers HIP regular, offset, array, 1D, 2D, 3D, cube/cube-array diagnostics, and multisample texel fetch/query forms with deterministic coordinate, offset, resource-role, and target-shape diagnostics.

mojo.notes: For the repo-owned compile-oriented Mojo contract, regular, offset, and multisample texel fetch forms lower to compile-smoke placeholder helpers with split-sampler handling and deterministic resource, coordinate, offset, sample/lod, and argument diagnostics.

rust.notes: For the compile-oriented Rust placeholder backend, regular, offset, multisample, explicit sampler, and HLSL Texture.Load texel fetch forms lower to Rust placeholder helpers with compile-smoke coverage.

slang.notes: Covers 1D, 1D-array, 2D, 2D-array, 3D, multisample, explicit-sampler, resource-array, control-flow, and loop texelFetch lowering to Slang Load or multisample subscript forms, plus fetch-offset lowering and deterministic invalid resource, coordinate, offset, fetch-index, and target-type diagnostics. Cube/cube-array fetches are rejected as target-invalid forms.

features.name: Multisample texture operations
features.description: Lower multisample texture fetch, query, and comparison forms with deterministic diagnostics for unsupported combinations.

directx.notes: Covers 2D and 2D-array multisample fetches, sample-count and level queries, resource arrays, and deterministic diagnostics for unsupported sample, offset, LOD, compare, and gather combinations.

opengl.notes: Covers 2D and 2D-array multisample fetches, sample-count and level queries, resource arrays, and deterministic diagnostics for unsupported sample, offset, LOD, compare, and gather combinations.

metal.notes: Covers 2D and 2D-array multisample fetches, sample-count and level queries, resource arrays, and deterministic diagnostics for unsupported sample, offset, LOD, compare, and gather combinations.

vulkan.notes: Covers 2D and 2D-array multisample fetches, sample-count/size queries, sample operand ordering, and deterministic diagnostics for unsupported offset, LOD/query-LOD, gather, and compare forms.

cuda.notes: Covers CUDA multisample sampled textures as texture-object resources with sample-count and size metadata queries, HLSL multisample aliases, and deterministic diagnostics for unsupported sampling, LOD, offset, gather, compare, and fetch forms that lack a CUDA texture-object lowering in this translator.

hip.notes: Covers HIP multisample sampled textures as texture-object resources with sample-count and size metadata queries plus deterministic diagnostics for unsupported sampling, LOD, offset, gather, compare, and fetch forms that lack a HIP texture-object lowering in this translator.

mojo.notes: For the repo-owned compile-oriented Mojo contract, multisample texture fetch and query forms lower to compile-smoke placeholder helpers, and advanced sampling/gather/compare forms on multisample textures emit deterministic diagnostics.

rust.notes: For the compile-oriented Rust placeholder backend, multisample sampled textures lower to Texture2DMS/Texture2DMSArray resources with texel-fetch, size, and sample-count helper calls that smoke compile.

slang.notes: Covers multisample texture fetches, sample-count and size queries, valid sample index handling, and deterministic diagnostics for unsupported multisample sampling, LOD/query-LOD, gather, and compare forms.

features.name: Advanced texture operations
features.description: LOD, gradients, offsets, projected coordinates, gathers, compares, and resource queries.

directx.notes: Covers LOD, gradients, offsets, projected coordinates, gathers, compares, resource queries, multisample query/fetch forms, resource arrays, and deterministic diagnostics for target-invalid advanced combinations.

opengl.notes: Covers LOD, gradients, offsets, projected coordinates, gathers, compares, resource queries, multisample query/fetch forms, resource arrays, and deterministic diagnostics for target-invalid advanced combinations.

metal.notes: Covers LOD, gradients, offsets, projected coordinates, gathers, compares, resource queries, multisample query/fetch forms, resource arrays, and deterministic diagnostics for target-invalid advanced combinations.

vulkan.notes: Covers regular and shadow sampling, LOD, gradient, offset, projected sampling, fragment bias operands, gather, shadow compare, texel fetch, query, multisample fetch/query forms, target-invalid diagnostics, and spirv-as/spirv-val validation coverage.

cuda.notes: Covers CUDA regular sampling, LOD, gradients, offsets, projected sampling, gather, shadow-compare diagnostics, texel fetch, query, multisample metadata/diagnostics, typed HLSL Sample* and Load/GetDimensions helpers, resource arrays, and deterministic invalid resource/argument forms.

hip.notes: Covers HIP regular sampling, LOD, gradients, projected sampling, gather, shadow-compare diagnostics, texel fetch, query, multisample metadata/diagnostics, resource arrays, and deterministic invalid resource/argument forms.

mojo.notes: For the repo-owned compile-oriented Mojo contract, advanced texture operations lower to compile-smoke placeholder helpers across LOD, gradients, offsets, projection, gather, compare, and query families, with deterministic diagnostics for image/non-texture targets, multisample targets for advanced sampling, and non-shadow compare targets.

rust.notes: For the compile-oriented Rust placeholder backend, advanced texture operations lower to helper calls across explicit LOD, gradients, offsets, projected coordinates, gathers, compares, texel fetches, resource queries, multisample fetch/query forms, explicit samplers, and HLSL Texture/SamplerState member aliases, with deterministic invalid-resource diagnostics.

slang.notes: Covers LOD, gradients, offsets, projected coordinates, gathers, compares, resource queries, multisample diagnostics, and deterministic invalid resource/argument forms through Slang texture helper lowering.

features.name: Storage image load/store
features.description: Readable and writable image resources with explicit formats and access qualifiers.

directx.notes: Covers typed RWTexture load/store for scalar, vector, narrow, explicit-format, array, access-qualified, and rasterizer-ordered images, with deterministic diagnostics for incompatible access.

opengl.notes: Covers image load/store for scalar, vector, narrow, explicit-format, array, and access-qualified images, with deterministic diagnostics for incompatible access.

metal.notes: Covers texture read/write for scalar, vector, narrow, explicit-format, array, and access-qualified images, with deterministic diagnostics for incompatible access.

vulkan.notes: Covers OpImageRead/OpImageWrite for scalar, vector, explicit-format, array, and multisample storage images, with spirv-as/spirv-val coverage for resource-heavy compute shaders.

cuda.notes: Covers CUDA storage images as surface-object resources with load/store lowering for 2D, 2D-array, 3D, typed HLSL writable aliases, arrays, nested resource arrays, access-qualified diagnostics, and deterministic coordinate/resource-shape diagnostics.

hip.notes: Covers HIP storage images as surface-object resources with load/store lowering for 1D, 1D-array, 2D, 2D-array, 3D, cube, cube-array, HLSL writable aliases, nested resource arrays, access-qualified diagnostics, and deterministic coordinate/resource-shape diagnostics.

mojo.notes: For the repo-owned compile-oriented Mojo contract, storage image load/store and size operations lower to compile-smoke placeholder helpers for float, signed, unsigned, and multisample images; HLSL RWTexture and RasterizerOrderedTexture aliases plus member Load/Store/GetDimensions lower to those helpers with deterministic argument diagnostics.

rust.notes: For the compile-oriented Rust placeholder backend, storage images lower to typed Image* placeholder resources with image_load/image_store, image_size/image_samples, explicit format value types, arrays, and structured-buffer helper interop that smoke compile.

slang.notes: Covers RWTexture load/store for scalar, vector, explicit-format, array, formatted-image, and multisample storage images using Slang subscript access, plus typed helper generation and deterministic readonly/writeonly and malformed load/store diagnostics.

features.name: Image atomics
features.description: Atomic operations on image resources.

directx.notes: Covers integer image atomic add/min/max/bitwise/exchange/compare-exchange across supported dimensions, including rasterizer-ordered textures, and emits deterministic diagnostics for unsupported formats and multisample atomics.

opengl.notes: Covers integer image atomic add/min/max/bitwise/exchange/compare-exchange across supported dimensions and emits deterministic diagnostics for unsupported formats and argument shapes.

metal.notes: Covers integer texture atomics add/min/max/bitwise/exchange/compare-exchange across supported dimensions and emits deterministic diagnostics for unsupported formats and multisample atomics.

vulkan.notes: Covers integer storage-image atomic add/min/max/bitwise/exchange/compare-exchange through OpImageTexelPointer and OpAtomic instructions, with spirv-as/spirv-val coverage.

cuda.notes: CUDA image atomic calls emit deterministic diagnostics or safe zero-value expressions for unsupported surface atomic semantics, with coordinate, format, access-mode, and multisample cases covered so source atomics never leak as invalid CUDA calls.

hip.notes: HIP image atomic calls emit deterministic diagnostics or safe zero-value expressions for unsupported surface atomic semantics, with coordinate, format, access-mode, cubemap, and multisample cases covered so source atomics never leak as invalid HIP calls.

mojo.notes: For the repo-owned compile-oriented Mojo contract, integer image atomic add/min/max/bitwise/exchange/compare-exchange forms lower to compile-smoke placeholder helpers, member aliases lower to the same helpers, float-image atomics are rejected, and readonly/writeonly image atomics emit deterministic diagnostics.

rust.notes: For the compile-oriented Rust placeholder backend, integer image atomic add/min/max/bitwise/exchange/compare-exchange helpers lower to typed Rust placeholder calls for scalar, vector, array, and multisample image resources with smoke compilation coverage.

slang.notes: Covers integer image atomic add/min/max/bitwise/exchange/compare-exchange across supported RWTexture dimensions through Slang Interlocked helper methods, direct AtomicOpNode lowering, expression-valued atomic preludes where evaluation order is preserved, loop-context diagnostics where hoisting would change semantics, image arrays, deterministic readonly/writeonly access diagnostics, target-type validation, float atomic diagnostics, and unsupported multisample/vector-format diagnostics.

features.name: Multisample storage images
features.description: Represent multisample storage images and report unsupported load/store/atomic combinations deterministically.

directx.notes: Represents multisample storage images as read-capable SRVs, preserves size/sample queries and arrays, and emits deterministic diagnostics for DirectX-unsupported stores and atomics.

opengl.notes: Covers multisample image load/store, sample-count and size queries, arrays, typed formats, and integer atomics where GLSL permits them.

metal.notes: Represents multisample storage images as read-capable textures, preserves size/sample queries and arrays, and emits deterministic diagnostics for Metal-unsupported stores and atomics.

vulkan.notes: Covers multisample storage-image load/store sample operands, sample-count queries, integer atomics where valid, deterministic malformed operand diagnostics, and spirv-as/spirv-val validation coverage.

cuda.notes: Represents CUDA multisample storage images as surface-object resources with metadata query forwarding and deterministic diagnostics for unsupported multisample load, store, and atomic operations.

hip.notes: Represents HIP multisample storage images as surface-object resources with metadata query forwarding and deterministic diagnostics for unsupported multisample load, store, and atomic operations.

mojo.notes: For the repo-owned compile-oriented Mojo contract, multisample image load/store placeholders, arrays, size/sample coordinates, HLSL multisample writable texture aliases, generic payloads, and image atomic placeholders compile through deterministic helper forms.

rust.notes: For the compile-oriented Rust placeholder backend, multisample storage images lower to Image2DMS/Image2DMSArray placeholder resources with sample-indexed load, store, query, and integer atomic helper calls that smoke compile.

slang.notes: Covers multisample storage-image load/store sample operands, sample-count and size queries, valid sample index handling, and deterministic diagnostics for malformed multisample image load/store calls.

features.name: Struct declarations and construction
features.description: Lower struct declarations, member access, initialization, and return values.

vulkan.notes: SPIR-V lowers struct type declarations, constructors, member access, nested member access, parameters, local copies, returns, and resource-bearing struct values to OpTypeStruct, OpCompositeConstruct, OpAccessChain/OpCompositeExtract, and layout-compatible local values with spirv-as/spirv-val coverage.

features.name: Array declarations and access
features.description: Lower fixed arrays, array parameters, indexing, and array return diagnostics.

vulkan.notes: SPIR-V lowers fixed arrays, array literals, local arrays, array parameters, array return values, nested array/struct member access, and dynamic indexing to OpTypeArray, OpCompositeConstruct, OpAccessChain, and local-copy helpers with spirv-as/spirv-val coverage.

features.name: Function declarations and calls
features.description: Lower helper functions, entry functions, call expressions, and return statements.

vulkan.notes: SPIR-V lowers non-generic helper functions, entry functions, function parameters, return-value conversion, argument conversion, user calls, and structured return statements to OpFunction/OpFunctionParameter/OpFunctionCall with spirv-as/spirv-val coverage. Generic functions emit deterministic diagnostics until SPIR-V monomorphization support is implemented.

cuda.notes: CUDA lowers non-generic helper functions, entry functions, calls, parameters, and returns. Generic functions emit deterministic diagnostics until CUDA monomorphization support is implemented.

hip.notes: HIP lowers non-generic helper functions, entry functions, calls, parameters, and returns. Generic functions emit deterministic diagnostics until HIP monomorphization support is implemented.

mojo.notes: Mojo lowers non-generic helper functions, entry functions, calls, parameters, and returns. Generic functions emit deterministic diagnostics until the backend has a generic ABI.

slang.notes: Slang lowers non-generic helper functions, entry functions, calls, parameters, and returns. Generic functions emit deterministic diagnostics until Slang monomorphization support is implemented.

features.name: Control flow
features.description: if/else, loops, switch, break, continue, and return statements.

vulkan.notes: SPIR-V lowers if/else, for, while, do-while, switch/default, break, continue, returns, and resource use inside structured control flow to structured OpSelectionMerge/OpLoopMerge regions with spirv-as/spirv-val coverage.

features.name: Synchronization and memory barriers
features.description: Lower barrier, memoryBarrier, and workgroupBarrier calls to backend-native synchronization intrinsics.

directx.notes: barrier/workgroupBarrier lower to GroupMemoryBarrierWithGroupSync; memoryBarrier lowers to AllMemoryBarrier; user-defined functions with the same names are preserved.

opengl.notes: workgroupBarrier lowers to GLSL barrier; barrier and memoryBarrier remain native; user-defined workgroupBarrier functions are preserved.

metal.notes: barrier/workgroupBarrier, groupMemoryBarrier, and memoryBarrierShared lower to threadgroup barriers; memoryBarrier, memoryBarrierBuffer, and deviceMemoryBarrier lower to device-memory barriers; memoryBarrierImage lowers to a texture-memory barrier; allMemoryBarrier lowers to combined device/threadgroup/texture flags; threadgroup atomic arrays and GLSL buffer-block atomic compare helpers use address-form atomic operations across helper/kernel barrier ordering; atomic compare-exchange lowers target and expected-value address forms to toolchain-compatible Metal intrinsics, with deterministic diagnostics for unsupported expected-pointer address spaces; user-defined functions with the same names are preserved.

vulkan.notes: barrier/workgroupBarrier lower to OpControlBarrier; groupMemoryBarrier and memoryBarrierShared lower to workgroup-scoped OpMemoryBarrier, memoryBarrierBuffer and memoryBarrierImage lower to device-scoped buffer/image memory barriers, memoryBarrier/allMemoryBarrier lower to device-scoped all-memory barriers, user-defined functions with synchronization names are preserved, and spirv-as/spirv-val coverage exists.

cuda.notes: Native CUDA barrier and cooperative-groups thread-block sync forms round-trip to CrossGL workgroup barriers, with unsupported non-workgroup cooperative scopes, cooperative-groups async copy/wait calls, CUDA barrier/pipeline async-copy helper calls, and CUDA primitive pipeline intrinsics emitted as expression-safe diagnostics.

hip.notes: barrier/workgroupBarrier lower to __syncthreads, memoryBarrier variants lower to HIP threadfence or sync helpers, argument misuse emits deterministic diagnostics, user-defined functions with synchronization names are preserved, and optional hipcc smoke coverage validates a barrier/atomic kernel when available.

mojo.notes: For the repo-owned compile-oriented Mojo contract, barrier/workgroupBarrier, memoryBarrier, and compute-shader synchronization forms lower to compile-smoke placeholder helpers, while user-defined functions with those names are preserved. Target-native GPU barrier semantics are outside the current Mojo placeholder contract.

rust.notes: For the compile-oriented Rust placeholder backend, barrier/workgroupBarrier, memoryBarrier* forms, and HLSL Group/Device/AllMemoryBarrier* aliases lower to compile-safe Rust helper calls while user-defined functions with those names remain preserved.

features.name: Wave/subgroup intrinsics
features.description: Lower CrossGL Wave* subgroup operations including lane queries, reductions, scans, votes, ballots, and lane reads.

directx.notes: WaveOpNode calls are preserved as native HLSL Wave* intrinsics across lane queries, votes, reductions, scans, ballots, WaveMatch, WaveMultiPrefix*, quad reads, and arity/type diagnostics. Optional DXC validation covers a compute wave shader when dxc is installed.

opengl.notes: Lowers CrossGL Wave* lane, vote, reduction, ballot, shuffle, quad, WaveMatch, and WaveMultiPrefix* operations to GL_KHR_shader_subgroup builtins or local helper functions with required extension declarations, deterministic arity/type diagnostics, and glslang validation coverage.

metal.notes: Lowers CrossGL Wave* operations to Metal simdgroup intrinsics or helper functions, threads compute-stage lane metadata through helper calls, and emits compile-safe diagnostics for contexts where Metal cannot supply simdgroup lane values.

vulkan.notes: Lowers WaveGetLaneCount, WaveGetLaneIndex, WaveIsFirstLane, WaveActiveSum/Product/Min/Max/BitAnd/BitOr/BitXor, WavePrefixSum/Product, WaveActiveAllTrue/AnyTrue/Ballot, WaveReadLaneAt, WaveReadLaneFirst, QuadRead*, WaveMatch, and WaveMultiPrefix* to SPIR-V GroupNonUniform operations with spirv-as/spirv-val coverage where tools are available. Dynamic or out-of-range quad lanes and invalid multi-prefix masks emit deterministic diagnostics.

cuda.notes: CUDA lowers Wave* and Quad* forms to compile-smoke subgroup helper placeholders for lane queries, reductions, votes, ballots, lane reads, quad reads, WaveMatch, and WaveMultiPrefix* forms, with optional nvcc compile coverage when nvcc is available.

hip.notes: HIP lowers Wave* and Quad* forms to compile-smoke subgroup helper placeholders for lane queries, reductions, votes, ballots, lane reads, quad reads, WaveMatch, and WaveMultiPrefix* forms, with optional hipcc compile coverage when hipcc is available.

mojo.notes: For the repo-owned compile-oriented Mojo contract, Wave* and Quad* forms lower to compile-smoke placeholder helpers for lane queries, reductions, votes, ballots, lane reads, quad reads, WaveMatch, and WaveMultiPrefix* forms. Target-native subgroup ABI semantics are outside the current Mojo placeholder contract.

rust.notes: For the compile-oriented Rust placeholder backend, Wave* and Quad* subgroup operations lower to typed Rust helper calls for lane queries, reductions, votes, ballots, lane reads, quad reads, WaveMatch, WaveMultiPrefix*, WaveShuffle, and WaveShuffleXor with smoke compilation coverage.

slang.notes: Lowers WaveGetLaneCount, WaveGetLaneIndex, WaveIsFirstLane, WaveActiveSum/Product/Min/Max/BitAnd/BitOr/BitXor, WavePrefixSum/Product, WaveActiveAllTrue/AnyTrue/Ballot, WaveReadLaneAt, WaveReadLaneFirst, QuadRead*, WaveMatch, and WaveMultiPrefix* forms to native Slang/HLSL wave intrinsics with deterministic arity diagnostics. slangc-backed coverage is optional and skips when slangc is unavailable locally.

features.name: Match/pattern lowering
features.description: CrossGL match expressions and pattern arms.

directx.notes: Covers switch-lowerable literal/wildcard arms, guarded if-chain arms, identifier bindings, struct fields, enum path and payload patterns, generic enum matches, match expressions, scoped case bodies, and shader validation with texture/image resources.

opengl.notes: Covers switch-lowerable literal/wildcard arms, guarded if-chain arms, identifier bindings, struct fields, enum path and payload patterns, generic enum matches, match expressions, scoped case bodies, and shader validation with texture/image resources.

metal.notes: Covers switch-lowerable literal/wildcard arms, guarded if-chain arms, identifier bindings, struct fields, enum path and payload patterns, generic enum matches, match expressions, scoped case bodies, and shader validation with texture/image resources.

vulkan.notes: SPIR-V lowers literal/wildcard arms, guarded arms, identifier bindings, struct field patterns, enum path and payload patterns, generic payload enum patterns, match expressions, returns, and scoped case bodies through structured selection chains with spirv-as/spirv-val coverage.

cuda.notes: CUDA lowers literal/wildcard arms, guarded arms, identifier bindings, scoped fallthrough bodies, struct field patterns, match expressions, typed temporaries, return match expressions, switch-style literal cases, wildcard defaults, complex bodies, and return arms.

hip.notes: HIP lowers literal/wildcard arms, guarded arms, identifier bindings, scoped fallthrough bodies, struct field patterns, match expressions, typed temporaries, return match expressions, switch-style literal cases, wildcard defaults, and deterministic diagnostics for unsupported constructor patterns.

mojo.notes: Lowers literal/wildcard condition chains, guarded literal arms, guarded identifier bindings, enum identifier patterns, payload enum destructuring, unqualified payload enum patterns, generic enum match expressions, exhaustive payload matches, final struct destructuring arms, match expressions, and compile-smoke struct destructuring. Unsupported incompatible and nonfinal-irrefutable patterns remain deterministic diagnostics.

rust.notes: Rust lowering covers literal/wildcard arms, guarded arms, identifier bindings, struct fields, enum path and payload patterns, generic enum matches, struct-like enum variants, target-typed struct-like generic enum constructor inference, match expressions, scoped case bodies, and non-Copy generic enum payload move/clone planning.

slang.notes: Lowers literal/wildcard arms, guarded arms, identifier bindings, scoped fallthrough bodies, struct field patterns, payload enum patterns, generic enum match expressions, match expressions, missing-value-path defaults, and deterministic diagnostics for unsupported guarded/range typed-value patterns.

features.name: Vector and matrix expressions
features.description: Vector/matrix constructors, arithmetic, swizzles, casts, and helper intrinsics.

vulkan.notes: SPIR-V codegen lowers scalar/vector/bool constructors, vector constructor flattening and arity diagnostics, matrix constructors including double and mixed-precision conversions, vector arithmetic and swizzle reads/writes/compound assignments, matrix add/subtract/scalar multiply/scalar divide/matrix multiply/matrix-vector/vector-matrix arithmetic, and vector helper intrinsics with validator-backed coverage.

cuda.notes: CUDA lowering emits concrete float/double matrix helper structs for 2x2 through 4x4 shapes alongside native vector aliases, so generated matrix type names and scalar constructors are self-contained.

hip.notes: HIP lowering emits concrete float/double matrix helper structs for 2x2 through 4x4 shapes alongside native vector aliases, so generated matrix type names and scalar constructors are self-contained.

features.name: Bitwise operations
features.description: Integer bitwise operators and assignment variants.

vulkan.notes: SPIR-V codegen lowers integer bitwise &, |, ^, shifts, unary bitwise not, and bitwise compound assignments to validated SPIR-V bitwise/shift opcodes.

features.name: Invalid shader shape validation
features.description: Backend-specific validation for invalid semantics, stages, resource shapes, and builtin types.

metal.notes: Metal codegen validates geometry maxvertexcount/input primitive shapes, tessellation patch metadata and domain-location shapes, and invalid texture, image, resource-array, and resource-query shapes with deterministic errors or compile-safe diagnostics.

vulkan.notes: SPIR-V codegen emits deterministic diagnostics or validated fallback modules for invalid builtin operand shapes, compute/stage metadata, tessellation patch contexts, geometry stream calls, mesh output counts and member/index targets, ray-query argument shapes, interface locations/components, duplicate resource bindings, malformed buffer/image/texture/resource-query operands, and dynamic resource-array shape conflicts.

cuda.notes: CUDA codegen validates geometry maxvertexcount/input primitive shapes, tessellation outputcontrolpoints/domain/partitioning metadata, mesh/task output topology, local-size metadata, SetMeshOutputCounts/DispatchMesh shapes, and invalid texture, image, resource-array, and resource-query shapes with deterministic errors, emitted helpers, or compile-safe diagnostics.

hip.notes: HIP codegen validates geometry maxvertexcount/input primitive shapes, tessellation outputcontrolpoints/domain/partitioning metadata, mesh/task output topology, local-size metadata, SetMeshOutputCounts/DispatchMesh shapes, and invalid texture, image, resource-array, and resource-query shapes with deterministic errors, emitted helpers, or compile-safe diagnostics.

mojo.notes: Mojo codegen validates unsupported payload enum shapes, payload constructor arity, parameter/return semantics, struct builtin semantics, geometry and mesh/task stage metadata, pipeline IR shapes, resource-size and expression target shapes, duplicate resource bindings, invalid texture/image/buffer operations, ray/mesh intrinsic contexts, ray-query methods, buffer counter/atomic methods, and unsupported match pattern shapes with deterministic errors or compile-safe diagnostics.

rust.notes: Rust codegen validates geometry maxvertexcount/input primitive shapes, tessellation outputcontrolpoints/domain/partitioning metadata, mesh/task output topology, local-size metadata, output parameter roles, SetMeshOutputCounts/DispatchMesh shapes, rejects unsupported ray stages with deterministic errors, keeps odd-but-valid empty/no-stage shaders compile-safe, and maps stage parameter semantics through tested Rust attributes/comments.

slang.notes: Slang codegen validates invalid tessellation patch shapes, mesh payload parameters, ray intrinsic result shapes, stage semantic parameters, stage attributes, and return semantics with deterministic errors or compile-safe diagnostics.

features.name: Project diagnostics
features.description: Emit structured project diagnostics for scan, translation, and validation failures.

directx.notes: Project diagnostics use compiler-compatible severity, code, message, location, target, and missing-capability fields while keeping per-artifact failures in the report. Scan/report CLI commands return nonzero when project reports contain error diagnostics, unsupported target names and non-repository-relative scan patterns including drive-qualified Windows paths are reported before validation, and validation rejects malformed preserved diagnostics, non-repository-relative diagnostic locations, inconsistent diagnostic location spans, or diagnostics whose targets are not declared by the report before copying them into validation output. Report summaries include contract-checked rollups by diagnostic code and missing capability, including duplicate external corpus manifest entry warnings.

features.name: External corpus coverage
features.description: Track repository-scale coverage against external shader and GPU source corpora.

directx.notes: Project reports can attach a repository-relative external corpus manifest, skip malformed or duplicate manifest entries with structured diagnostics, and summarize declared valid paths, present and missing entries, discovered translation units, source-backend, target, and artifact rollups for manifest-backed project runs. Validation checks entry presence/discovery/source-backend consistency, and text inspection surfaces the same rollups. The pinned reduced-corpus manifest includes a focused DirectX reduction covered by backend fixture tests. This is a coverage and accounting layer only: CrossTL does not clone upstream repositories, run native build systems, or claim whole-corpus semantic parity from reduced backend fixtures.
